question: [File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: flashLoan + safeTransfer loop] Can an unprivileged attacker under a Midnight deployment call flashLoan(tokens, assets, callback, data) where tokens contains N distinct token addresses each with code.length > 0, causing SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer to perform N extcodesize checks (each costing 2600 gas cold) plus N external calls, violating the invariant that flashLoan gas cost must be bounded by a function of the number of tokens, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS where an attacker can make flashLoan prohibitively expensive by choosing N large enough to exhaust the block gas limit? Proof idea: call flashLoan with N=500 distinct token addresses, measure gas, assert OOG at N=block_gas_limit/2600.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: liquidate (bad debt path, lines 626-641)] Can an unprivileged liquidator under a market with totalUnits approaching 0 call liquidate with seizedAssets=0 and repaidUnits=0 to realize bad debt, causing the lossFactor update computation (line 631-633) to execute without any SafeTransferLib calls, but then a subsequent take call to revert with MarketLossFactorMaxedOut (line 349) if lossFactor reaches type(uint128).max, violating the invariant that bad debt realization must not permanently freeze the market, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where all take operations in the market become permanently blocked after lossFactor is maxed out? Proof idea: repeatedly call liquidate(0, 0) until lossFactor == type(uint128).max, then call take and assert MarketLossFactorMaxedOut.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: multicall + withdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower under a market with 16 activated collaterals compose a multicall with N withdrawCollateral calls each for a different collateral index, causing N isHealthy calls (each looping 16 times over the collateral bitmap) plus N SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer calls, violating the invariant that multicall gas cost must not be amplified by the number of activated collaterals, causing scoped impact: gas griefing where a borrower with 16 activated collaterals makes multicall-based collateral withdrawal prohibitively expensive? Proof idea: fuzz N from 1 to 16 with 16 activated collaterals, measure multicall gas for withdrawCollateral.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: safeTransfer (in withdrawCollateral, line 572)] Can an unprivileged borrower under a market where the collateral token is a rebasing ERC20 (balance decreases over time, e.g., slashing) call withdrawCollateral(market, collateralIndex, assets, onBehalf, receiver), causing SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, assets) to attempt to transfer more than Midnight's actual balance of the collateral token, violating the invariant that Midnight's collateral balance must always cover recorded collateral positions, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where withdrawCollateral reverts because the actual token balance is less than the recorded collateral amount? Proof idea: deploy a slashing collateral token, supply 100 assets, slash 10%, call withdrawCollateral(100), assert revert.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: multicall + take + repay] Can an unprivileged taker under a Midnight deployment compose a multicall that first calls take (triggering 2 SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom calls) and then calls repay (triggering 1 SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom call) in the same transaction, causing the combined gas cost to exceed the block gas limit when the market has 128 collateral params and the seller has 16 activated collaterals, violating the invariant that common user flows must be composable within a single block, causing scoped impact: gas griefing where legitimate multicall compositions become impossible in high-collateral markets? Proof idea: measure gas for multicall([take, repay]) with 128-collateral market and 16-collateral seller, assert it fits within 30M gas.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: safeTransferFrom (in take, line 455)] Can an unprivileged taker under a market where the loan token is an ERC777 with a tokensToSend hook call take with takerCallback=address(0), causing SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets) to trigger the ERC777 tokensToSend hook on the payer, which reenters Midnight (e.g., calling take or withdraw), violating the invariant that SafeTransferLib calls must not allow reentrancy that corrupts partial state, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption where the reentrant call observes and exploits the partially updated position state before the take completes? Proof idea: deploy an ERC777 loan token with a hook that reenters take, call take, assert position state is consistent after both calls.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: safeTransfer (in liquidate, line 696)] Can an unprivileged liquidator under a market where the collateral token is an ERC777 with a tokensReceived hook call liquidate, causing SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, seizedAssets) to trigger the ERC777 tokensReceived hook on the receiver, which reenters Midnight (e.g., calling liquidate again on the same borrower), violating the invariant that liquidation must not allow reentrancy that double-seizes collateral or double-reduces debt, causing scoped impact: direct loss where the reentrant liquidation seizes collateral a second time before the first liquidation's safeTransferFrom has been called? Proof idea: deploy an ERC777 collateral token with a hook that reenters liquidate, call liquidate, assert collateral balance and debt are consistent.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: touchMarket (collateral list loop, line 762)] Can an unprivileged market creator under a Midnight deployment call touchMarket with market.collateralParams.length = 128 where each collateral token address is a contract with code.length > 0 but the oracle address is a contract that consumes high gas in price(), causing the touchMarket loop to complete (no oracle calls in touchMarket), but every subsequent isHealthy and liquidate call to consume O(16 * oracle_gas) gas, violating the invariant that market creation must not allow parameters that make subsequent operations prohibitively expensive, causing scoped impact: gas griefing where a market creator can make all health checks and liquidations in the market permanently expensive? Proof idea: create a market with 128 collaterals each having an oracle that consumes 100_000 gas, activate 16 collaterals, measure isHealthy gas.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: flashLoan (safeTransfer loop, line 742-744)] Can an unprivileged attacker under a Midnight deployment call flashLoan(tokens, assets, callback, data) where tokens contains addresses with code.length == 0 (EOAs), causing SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer to revert with NoCode() on the first such token, but only after the callback has already been called (line 746) and potentially executed state changes, violating the invariant that flashLoan must not allow partial execution where the callback runs but the repayment loop fails, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption where the callback's state changes are reverted but any off-chain effects (e.g., signed messages, oracle updates) are not? Proof idea: call flashLoan with a valid token followed by an EOA address, assert the callback is never called if the first safeTransfer fails.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: multicall + flashLoan (nested loops)] Can an unprivileged attacker under a Midnight deployment compose a multicall(calls) where calls[0] encodes flashLoan(tokens, assets, callback, data) with tokens.length = 1000, and calls[1..N] encode additional flashLoan calls, causing the multicall loop (line 212) to execute N flashLoan calls each with 1000 SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer + safeTransferFrom calls, violating the invariant that multicall must not allow O(N*M) external call amplification, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS where a single multicall transaction with N=10 and M=1000 triggers 20_000 external ERC20 calls? Proof idea: measure gas for multicall([flashLoan(1000 tokens)] * 10) and assert OOG.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: safeTransfer (in claimContinuousFee, line 324)] Can an unprivileged fee claimer under a Midnight deployment call claimContinuousFee(market, amount, receiver) where the loan token is a contract that consumes high gas in transfer(), causing SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(loanToken, receiver, amount) to consume excessive gas after the state updates to continuousFeeCredit, totalUnits, and withdrawable have already been applied, violating the invariant that fee claiming must complete atomically, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where the fee claimer cannot claim continuous fees due to the loan token's expensive transfer, while the accounting state has already been decremented? Proof idea: deploy a loan token whose transfer() consumes 500_000 gas, call claimContinuousFee, assert the transaction reverts and state is unchanged.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: isHealthy (bitmap loop) + safeTransfer (in withdrawCollateral)] Can an unprivileged borrower under a market with 16 activated collaterals call withdrawCollateral for a collateral that is not the last in the bitmap, causing isHealthy to loop over all 16 remaining collaterals (including the just-withdrawn one which now has 0 balance), and then SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer to transfer the withdrawn assets, violating the invariant that isHealthy must correctly account for the updated collateral balance after withdrawal, causing scoped impact: unauthorized seizure where the health check uses the pre-withdrawal collateral balance, allowing withdrawal of collateral that would make the position unhealthy? Proof idea: withdraw collateral such that post-withdrawal debt > maxDebt, assert UnhealthyBorrower is thrown.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: multicall + supplyCollateral + take] Can an unprivileged taker under a Midnight deployment compose a multicall that calls supplyCollateral (triggering SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom for collateral) followed by take (triggering 2 SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom for loan token), causing the combined gas cost to include both the collateral transfer and the loan token transfer plus the isHealthy check at the end of take, violating the invariant that multicall compositions must not create gas costs that exceed block limits for common user flows, causing scoped impact: gas griefing where a borrower who wants to supply collateral and immediately borrow in one transaction is blocked by OOG in high-collateral markets? Proof idea: measure gas for multicall([supplyCollateral, take]) with 128-collateral market.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: safeTransferFrom (in take, line 456)] Can an unprivileged taker under a market where the loan token returns false from transferFrom() (non-reverting false return) cause SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom to revert with TransferFromReturnedFalse() (line 33), but only after the buyer and seller position state has already been updated (lines 408-414) and the claimableSettlementFee has been incremented (line 418), violating the invariant that token transfer failure must revert all state changes atomically, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption where position state is updated but no tokens are transferred? Proof idea: deploy a loan token whose transferFrom() returns false, call take, assert the full transaction reverts and position state is unchanged.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: liquidate (collateral bitmap while-loop, line 607)] Can an unprivileged borrower under a market with MAX_COLLATERALS=128 activate 16 collaterals where each collateral has a different oracle, and then call liquidate with collateralIndex pointing to a collateral that is NOT in the activated bitmap, causing the while loop to compute maxDebt and badDebt without including the liquidated collateral's price (liquidatedCollatPrice remains 0), and then the repaidUnits computation (line 650: seizedAssets.mulDivUp(0, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivUp(WAD, lif)) to return 0, violating the invariant that liquidation must correctly compute repaidUnits from the seized collateral's price, causing scoped impact: direct loss where a liquidator seizes collateral without repaying any debt? Proof idea: call liquidate with collateralIndex not in collateralBitmap and seizedAssets > 0, assert repaidUnits > 0.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: multicall + take (payer confusion)] Can an unprivileged taker under a Midnight deployment compose a multicall where call[0] sets up a state where the payer for a subsequent take is a contract that has been drained of tokens, causing SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), buyerAssets - sellerAssets) in call[1] to revert, violating the invariant that multicall must atomically revert all calls if any call fails, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption where the position state updated in call[1] before the safeTransferFrom is not reverted if the multicall error handling is incorrect? Proof idea: compose multicall([drain_payer, take]) and assert the full multicall reverts atomically.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: safeTransfer (in withdraw, line 499) + multicall] Can an unprivileged lender under a Midnight deployment compose a multicall with N withdraw calls each for 1 unit to the same receiver, causing N SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(loanToken, receiver, 1) calls, and if the loan token is an ERC777 with a tokensReceived hook on the receiver, causing N reentrant calls into Midnight from the receiver's hook, violating the invariant that multicall must not allow reentrancy that corrupts the sequential state updates across calls, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption where the reentrant calls observe intermediate state between multicall iterations? Proof idea: deploy an ERC777 loan token with a receiver hook that reenters withdraw, compose multicall([withdraw]*N), assert credit balance is consistent.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: touchMarket (collateral list loop) + isHealthy (bitmap loop)] Can an unprivileged market creator under a Midnight deployment create a market with 128 collateral params where collateral indices 0-15 are valid tokens and oracles, and a borrower activates all 16 allowed collaterals (indices 0-15), causing isHealthy to loop 16 times calling oracle.price() for each, and then a taker calling take against this borrower-as-seller to trigger isHealthy at line 476, violating the invariant that take gas cost must be bounded regardless of the seller's collateral configuration, causing scoped impact: gas griefing where a seller with 16 activated collaterals in a 128-collateral market makes every take against them prohibitively expensive? Proof idea: create a 128-collateral market, activate 16 collaterals for the seller, call take, measure gas.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: safeTransferFrom (in take, lines 455-456)] Can an unprivileged taker under a market where the loan token is a contract that returns a 32-byte true value from transferFrom() (standard ERC20) but also emits a very large event log cause SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom to succeed (returndata.length == 32, abi.decode returns true) while the large event log increases the transaction gas cost, violating the invariant that SafeTransferLib must not be vulnerable to gas griefing through token event logs, causing scoped impact: gas griefing where a malicious loan token emits large events on every transfer, making take prohibitively expensive? Proof idea: deploy a token that emits a 10KB event on transferFrom, call take, measure gas overhead from event logs.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: liquidate (safeTransfer before safeTransferFrom)] Can an unprivileged liquidator under a market call liquidate with a callback that reenters liquidate on the same borrower (since the liquidation lock is set via tExchange in take but NOT in liquidate), causing the reentrant liquidate call to observe the already-updated position state (debt reduced, collateral reduced) and potentially seize more collateral or realize bad debt a second time, violating the invariant that liquidation must not allow reentrancy that double-reduces debt or double-seizes collateral, causing scoped impact: direct loss where the reentrant liquidation seizes collateral without a corresponding debt reduction? Proof idea: deploy a liquidate callback that reenters liquidate, call liquidate, assert position.debt and position.collateral are consistent after both calls.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: multicall + flashLoan + supplyCollateral] Can an unprivileged attacker under a Midnight deployment compose a multicall where call[0] is flashLoan (sending tokens to a callback), call[1] is supplyCollateral (triggering safeTransferFrom), and call[2] is take (triggering 2 safeTransferFrom calls), causing the combined gas cost of 4 SafeTransferLib calls plus the flashLoan loop plus the isHealthy loop to exceed block gas limits in a 128-collateral market with 16-collateral seller, violating the invariant that common multicall compositions must fit within block gas limits, causing scoped impact: gas griefing where legitimate protocol flows become impossible in high-collateral markets? Proof idea: measure gas for multicall([flashLoan(100 tokens), supplyCollateral, take]) with 128-collateral market.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: safeTransfer (in liquidate, line 696)] Can an unprivileged liquidator under a market call liquidate with seizedAssets=0 and repaidUnits=0 (bad debt only path), causing SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, 0) to be called with value=0 (still performing extcodesize check + external call), violating the invariant that zero-value SafeTransferLib calls must not revert or cause unexpected behavior, causing scoped impact: fund freeze where bad debt realization fails if the collateral token reverts on zero-value transfers? Proof idea: deploy a collateral token that reverts on transfer(receiver, 0), call liquidate(0, 0), assert the bad debt realization fails.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: multicall (unbounded delegatecall loop)] Can an unprivileged attacker under a Midnight deployment call multicall(calls) where calls.length = type(uint256).max or a very large number, causing the for-loop (line 212: for (uint256 i = 0; i < calls.length; i++)) to attempt to iterate an astronomically large number of times, violating the invariant that multicall must revert gracefully on invalid input rather than consuming all block gas, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS where a single multicall call with a huge calls array exhausts the block gas limit? Proof idea: call multicall with calls.length = 10_000 and empty call data, measure gas, assert OOG at a predictable threshold.

[File: 'src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: safeTransferFrom (in take, line 455) + safeTransfer (in liquidate, line 696)] Can an unprivileged attacker under a market where the loan token and collateral token are the same address (same ERC20 used as both) cause SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom in take to pull loan tokens while SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer in liquidate sends the same token as collateral, creating a scenario where the token balance accounting conflates loan token reserves and collateral reserves, violating the invariant that loan token balance must cover withdrawable + claimableSettlementFee and collateral token balance must cover all collateral positions, causing scoped impact: protocol insolvency where the shared token balance is insufficient to cover both obligations? Proof idea: create a market with loanToken == collateralToken, execute borrows and liquidations, assert Midnight.balanceOf(token) >= withdrawable + sum(collateral).
